message:
test(fcp-manifest): pin SemVer parse boundaries for connector + min_mesh_version

Adds a table-driven boundary test for the SemVer fields that gate every
connector manifest load: `connector.version` and
`manifest.min_mesh_version`. These two fields are the only path through
which a manifest can express compatibility with the runtime, and a
parser drift here is silent — a malformed version that accidentally
parses (e.g. `01.2.3` accepted as 1.2.3 by a lenient parser) would let
non-conforming connectors pass admission.

Cases pinned:

VALID_VERSION_BOUNDARIES (must accept):
- "0.0.0", "1.0.0", "0.0.1" — minimal patch boundaries
- "1.2.3-rc.1" — pre-release identifier
- "1.2.3+build.42" — build metadata identifier

INVALID_VERSION_FORMS (must reject):
- ""           — empty
- "1", "1.2"   — missing components (semver requires major.minor.patch)
- "1.2.3.4"    — extra component
- "v1.2.3"     — leading "v" prefix
- "01.2.3", "1.02.3", "1.2.03" — leading zeros on numeric components
- "1.2.x"      — non-numeric patch
- "1.2.3-"     — empty pre-release identifier
- "1.2.3+"     — empty build identifier
- "1.2.3-rc..1", "1.2.3+build..42" — empty inner identifier
- "1.2.3-01"   — numeric pre-release identifier with leading zero

Test exercises both `Version::from_str` directly (so a future swap of
the underlying semver crate gets caught) and a full `ConnectorManifest`
load from a synthesised TOML payload (so wiring drift between the
manifest schema and the version parser also gets caught). The TOML
helper builds a minimal valid manifest with the version fields
substituted in, so the only thing the parse touches under test is the
SemVer field — every other manifest field is held constant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
